channel.title: Ad News Feed
channel.description: 広告プラットフォーム最新情報の自動収集フィード

item.title: [Google Ads] The "Slept On" Strategy to Scale Google Ads in 2026 [Inside Google Ads Episode 116]
item.description: Stop fighting for the same tiny audience as your competitors. If you've been running Google Ads for a while, you might notice that the old-school tactics of hyper-specific targeting are not performing like they used to. In episode 116 of the Inside Google Ads podcast, host Jyll Saskin Gales shares a

item.title: [Google Ads] Google Ads Certifications: Partner vs Premier Explained #shorts
item.description: Unlock Google Ads knowledge! Learn about certifications via Skillshop and the Google Partner Program. Understand requirements and if Premier Partner status is right for you. #GoogleAds #Skillshop #GooglePartner #DigitalMarketing #AdCertifications

item.description:
Today we’re announcing the April 2026 update to the Display & Video 360 API. This update includes the following:

item.title: [Microsoft Ads] Publisher release roundup: Q1 2026 edition
item.description: Check out our Q1 2026 Microsoft Monetize updates that are focused on delivering practical improvements across usability, deals, and ad quality

item.title: [Google Ads] PMAX, AI MAX & The Future of Google Ads
item.description: In this interview I am joined by 2 of my Senior Google Ads Growth Strategists from Define Digital Academy, Brent Neale and Johan Carlberger, as we break down what's working with Performance Max, the potential of AI Max, keyword cannibalization, bidding strategies and how AI is reshaping Google Ads.

item.title: [Google Ads] Google Ads Copy: Fast AI Method for Clients #shorts
item.description: Struggling with Google Ads copy? Use your client's landing page content to generate Performance Max ad copy instantly. A streamlined process for physical therapy practices. #GoogleAds #AdCopy #DigitalMarketing #PhysicalTherapy

item.title: [Meta Ads] Do You Need to Warm Up a New Ad Account?
item.description: Today's question is about whether a brand new ad account, Facebook page, and pixel need any special treatment before running sales campaigns. Conventional wisdom says to build followers first, run awareness campaigns, or season the pixel with top-of-funnel activity. Jon explains why these recommenda

item.title: [Google Ads] How to Create GA4 Audiences and Use Them in Google Ads
item.description: If you're running Google Ads, you can use audiences from Google Analytics to target your campaigns and reach the right people. In this tutorial, I'll walk you through how to create an audience in GA4, check that consent is configured correctly, and apply your audience in Google Ads. To do this, we'l

item.title: [Google Ads] We’re upgrading Dynamic Search Ads to AI Max
item.description: AI Max is moving out of beta, with improved performance quality across targeting and creative capabilities along with more enhanced controls.

item.title: [LINE Ads] 【2026年4月統合開始】LINEヤフー広告 ディスプレイ広告への移行ガイド。運用者がチェックすべきポイントを解説
item.description:
2026年4月に、従来の LINE 広告と Yahoo! 広告が統合され「LINE ヤフー広告 ディスプレイ広告」の提供が開始されました。 これに伴い、運用担当者やマーケターの方は「結局、自分は何か作業をしなければならな …

"【2026年4月統合開始】LINEヤフー広告 ディスプレイ広告への移行ガイド。運用者がチェック

item.title: [General] Scaling Paid Media Without Burning Cash: Smarter Testing Frameworks for Modern PPC
item.description: Scaling paid media looks simple, but what usually happens is this: you push spend, performance holds for a few days, then CPAs start creeping up.  Branded and high-intent campaigns carry the account for a while, but the moment you lean into broader queries or new audiences, efficiency drops.

item.title: [General] From Clicks to Confidence: How Brands Validate PPC Performance Without Flawed Attribution
item.description: Thanks to AI and platform tools, PPC campaigns can be created fairly quickly.  That’s the easy part. But how do you measure results and attribute clicks to the right campaign?   Cross-device journeys made things messy, then Privacy changes made things even worse. Platforms fill in

item.title: [Google Ads] Why Your Google Ads Aren’t Getting Clicks
item.description: If you’ve started running Google Ads and no one is clicking, it can feel frustrating. You might wonder if you’re doing something wrong or if ads just don’t work. The truth is, Google Ads do work, but small mistakes can stop them from performing. The good news is that most problems are easy to fix

item.title: [Google Ads] Google Keyword Planner: How to Find High-Intent Keywords, Estimate Search Demand, and Build Smarter SEO & PPC Campaigns
item.description: Google Keyword Planner has been around for years, but it still holds a unique place in keyword research because the data comes from Google Ads. That matters. When you want to understand how Google groups search demand, how advertisers value a query, and how keyword interest changes by location or

item.title: [General] Digital Agency Client Onboarding Checklist: 27 Best Practices, Questions, and Templates
item.description: Winning a new client is expensive. Keeping that client long enough to produce meaningful results is where agency economics start to work. That matters because “digital marketing” is not one skill. It is a stack. Search, pai

item.title: [General] Meta Conversations 2026 Date Announced: What the June 3 London Event Means for WhatsApp, Messenger, and Instagram Business Messaging
item.description: Meta has confirmed that Conversations 2026, its annual business messaging conference, will take place on June 3 in London, with a global livestream available for registered viewers. On the surface, that sounds like a straightforward event notice. In practice, it is more than that. Conversations h

item.title: [General] Bud Light NFL Draft Keg Wall 2026: How Free Draft Beer Works in Pittsburgh and 9 Partner Markets
item.description: Bud Light is tying one of the NFL calendar’s most watched offseason moments to a simple, easy-to-understand fan offer: when your team is on the clock, a draft beer is on Bud Light. That idea sits at the center of the brand’s 2026 NFL Draft activation in Pittsburgh, where Bud Light is introducing

item.description:

item.title: [General] Use Breakdowns for Troubleshooting
[General] SEO Pricing Guide 2026: How Much Does SEO Really Cost by Month, Project, and Business Size?
item.description: If you want the short answer first, most businesses in 2026 should expect SEO to cost anywhere from $1,500 to $5,000 per month for a serious small-to-midsize campaign, with local SEO often starting lower and national, ecommerce, SaaS, or enterprise campaigns climbing well beyond $7,500 per month.

item.description: Search has changed shape. For years, visibility mostly meant earning a blue link, climbing a rankings report, and hoping the searcher clicked through to a page. item.title: [General] What Is M&M’S POP’D Caramel? Inside the First Freeze-Dried M&M’S Candy and the BBDO Chicago Campaign
item.description: M&M’S has spent decades building one of the most recognizable formats in candy: a small, colorful shell, a familiar bite, and a product system people understand before they even open the bag. item.title: [Google Ads] Google Unifies Enhanced Conversions Settings in 2026: What Changes in April and June, What Advertisers Need to Do, and How to Prepare
item.description: Google Ads is simplifying one of the more confusing parts of conversion measurement. In 2026, Google is unifying enhanced conversions settings so advertisers no longer have to think about enhanced conversions for web and enhanced conversions for leads as separate setup paths inside the interface.
